evidence: Some of our product sales are tied to artificial intelligence (AI)-related capital expenditures and data center infrastructure.

Some of our product sales are tied to artificial intelligence (AI)-related capital expenditures and data center infrastructure.

AI algorithms and training methodologies may be flawed. These deficiencies and other failures of AI systems could subject us to competitive harm, regulatory action, legal liability, and brand or reputational harm.

incorporating AI could give rise to litigation risk and risk of non-compliance and unknown cost of compliance, as AI is an emerging technology for which the legal and regulatory landscape is not fully developed

use of AI to support business operations carries inherent risks related to data privacy and security, such as intended, unintended, or inadvertent transmission of proprietary, sensitive or export-controlled information

limitations: ai_investment_intensity: No quantified AI-specific capex, headcount, or named vendor/infrastructure partnerships disclosed in the excerpts; company references AI-related customer capex as a revenue driver but discloses no own AI investment amounts.

ai_narrative_concreteness: AI use cases are referenced generically ('certain of our products and solutions') without naming specific deployed products, metrics, or deployment status; score of 3 reflects named but undetailed initiatives.

ai_risk_disclosure_depth: Risk-section material is present and contains detailed AI risk scenarios with named mechanisms (regulatory, liability, competitive, data privacy, export-control); scored 4 as mechanisms are described but no quantification or named governance body specifically for AI risk (Audit Committee covers cybersecurity broadly, not AI specifically).
